channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the White House to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump helps plant replacement for a historic White House tree that was removed over safety concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday helped shovel dirt onto the replacement for a historic White House tree that had to be removed because of safety concerns around its deteriorating condition. “We have a beautiful tree now at the White House,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China vows retaliation and a 'fight to the end' after Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: Trump said on Truth Social he would impose the new tariffs on China if Beijing did not retract a 34% retaliatory tariff on U.S. goods that it announced in response to Trump's initial salvo last Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Growing opposition from House conservatives threatens to derail Trump's agenda
item.description: Growing opposition among hard-line House conservatives to the Senate’s framework for advancing President Trump’s ambitious legislative agenda is threatening to make this week’s vote one of the heaviest lifts yet for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' web page
item.description: The changes were first noticed in a review by The Washington Post, which found that dozens of pages have been edited since President Trump took the oath of office.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday, as the outbreak continues to swell.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: Lawmakers are talking of clawing back their power to levy tariffs, but almost none seemed ready to turn their words into action. The Republican president is upending longstanding GOP principles like support for free trade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: For these seniors, DOGE's affordable housing pause means suffering longer without AC
item.description: The Trump administration froze funding for a program to upgrade aging low-income housing and make it energy efficient. The move threatens hundreds of projects around the country.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden, MPR News

item.title: Trump closes loophole used by American shoppers to buy Chinese goods tariff-free
item.description: The so-called de minimis exemption allowed Chinese and Hong Kong retailers to ship millions of packages worth $800 or less directly to U.S. consumers. That loophole will close May 2.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9
